meta_title: For Freelancers: client workflow to Solve no design skills (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome no design skills. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelance Client Workflow: Overcoming No Design Skills
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with design. Learn a professional freelance workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver high-end SaaS projects without design skills.
content.answer_block: To overcome a lack of design skills, adopt a code-first, component-driven workflow. Use Antigravity to generate high-fidelity UI foundations and Claude Code to iterate on layout logic. By treating design as a system of constraints rather than art, you deliver pixel-perfect, professional-grade SaaS applications at lightning speed.
content.intro:
The midnight oil is burning, your client’s deadline is looming, and you’re staring at a blank editor, paralyzed by the 'design gap.' You know how to architect robust backends, but your UI feels like a relic from the early 2000s. The industry demands sleek, modern interfaces, and your lack of formal design training has become the ultimate bottleneck to your revenue growth.

But here is the secret: top-tier SaaS delivery isn't about artistic talent; it's about engineering a repeatable design system. By leveraging AI-augmented development, you can bypass the 'design phase' entirely, transforming architectural constraints into aesthetic advantages. It’s time to stop pixel-pushing and start shipping scalable, high-converting interfaces through pure code-driven automation.

steps.title: Define UI Constraints with Antigravity
steps.description: Establish a rigid design system to prevent 'feature creep' and visual incoherence. By defining your palette and spacing scale first, you ensure every subsequent component remains consistent.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a CSS-in-JS theme object for a modern SaaS dashboard. Include a slate-based color palette, a 4px spacing scale, and a typography system using Inter. Output in TypeScript.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a theme.ts file based on the Antigravity output and apply it to the Tailwind config.'

steps.title: Component Architecture Setup
steps.description: Use Claude Code to scaffold a library of atomic components. Focusing on small, reusable pieces allows you to assemble complex pages like a professional UI kit.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a set of React components for a SaaS UI: Button, Input, Card, and Modal. Use Tailwind CSS and Headless UI patterns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a components/ui directory and implement the generated components with accessibility best practices.'

steps.title: Layout Engineering
steps.description: Stop manually centering divs. Use AI to construct the layout scaffolding that enforces responsive design without requiring manual CSS tweaking.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a responsive dashboard layout with a collapsible sidebar and a main content area using CSS Grid and Flexbox.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the dashboard layout in App.tsx, ensuring the sidebar state is managed via React Context.'

steps.title: Data-Driven UI Population
steps.description: Connect your mock data to the UI components. This ensures your design holds up under real-world data density, a common failure point for amateur designers.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a mock API service and a hook to fetch dummy user analytics data for the dashboard cards.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Integrate the mock data hook into the dashboard cards and add loading skeletons for improved UX.'

steps.title: Automated Design QA
steps.description: Use Claude Code to perform a final visual audit of your component library, checking for common alignment issues and mobile responsiveness gaps.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current codebase for common UI inconsistencies, such as mismatched padding or font sizes, and suggest a refactor.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze current CSS styles for inconsistencies and apply a standardized spacing utility class across all components.'

pro_tips: Always build in Dark Mode first; it hides minor spacing imperfections that are glaring in Light Mode.
Use 'semantic naming' for your variables (e.g., bg-action-primary) rather than literal colors (e.g., bg-blue-600) to allow for instant re-branding.
Limit your font stack to two weights and three sizes to instantly elevate the 'professionalism' of your UI.
Force Claude Code to write unit tests for your UI components to ensure the layout doesn't break during future refactors.
Use a 4px grid system religiously; it is the industry standard for creating a balanced, organized interface with zero design training.
content.next_steps: Once the foundation is set, integrate a headless CMS to allow your clients to update content without touching your code, effectively automating the 'maintenance' phase of your freelance contract.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical blueprint to ship professional SaaS projects without ever opening Figma. Stop letting design anxiety hold back your freelance business. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
